items.title: In Seremban, babysitter jailed one year, fined RM15,000 over death of ‘Baby Syifaa’ four years ago
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — A babysitter was sentenced to one year’s imprisonment by the Sessions Court in Seremban today for negligence that led to the death of 15-month-old “Baby Syifaa” four years ago. The court also imposed a RM15,000 fine, with an additional four months’ jail in default of payment. Also present in court were Baby Syifaa’s parents, Anida Idrus, 44, and Yuseri Yusoff, 42.

items.title: One man, his dog, and ChatGPT: Australia's AI vaccine saga
items.summary: Sydney-based AI consultant Conyngham told AFP that eight-year-old Rosie's mast cell cancer is now in partial remission and her biggest tumour has shrunk dramatically. Conyngham does not call his findings a cure – but experts unrelated to the dogged endeavour said they highlight AI's potential to accelerate medical research. "I would have conversations and just keep them going non-stop" with ChatGPT, Gemini and Grok to study cancer therapies in-depth, Conyngham said.

items.title: Penang says water supply secure for two months amid dry spell
items.summary: Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said the current reserves in the Muda Dam and Beris Dam in Kedah are sufficient to support water supply through April and May. He said the Muda Dam capacity has dropped to a critical 7.19 per cent as of March 28 and can no longer release water into Sungai Muda after significant drawdowns earlier this year for irrigation. The capacity of the 122,400 ML Beris Dam was at 100 per cent on January 1 and its capacity has dropped to 58.06 per cent as of March 28.

items.title: MMEA rescues 13 tourists after boat stalls at sea
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) rescued 13 tourists after their boat stalled off Kuala Besut yesterday afternoon. State MMEA director Captain Hamiludin Che Awang said the group, which included 12 foreigners, was rescued by an MMEA team on a routine patrol. He said the patrol team spotted the boat, approximately 7.3 nautical miles from the Kuala Besut estuary, drifting, and passengers waving for help.

items.title: KJ's suit against Jamal to be heard in Sessions Court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court here today ruled that former Health Minister Khairy Jamaluddin's application to file a lawsuit against Sungai Besar Umno division chief Jamal Yunos must be heard at the Sessions Court. Judge Datuk Anand Ponnudurai made the decision on the grounds that claims filed in the High Court must involve amounts exceeding RM1 million. Lawyer V. Mugunthan, representing Jamal as the defendant, said his client was prepared to issue an apology, although the plaintiff was also seeking damages.

items.title: Three week water disruption in Gua Musang’s Rantau Manis forces residents to bathe at R&R and rivers
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, March 30 — Some residents of Taman Agropolitan Rantau Manis here have been forced to bathe at highway rest and service areas (R&R), rivers, and transport water from the town located about 40 kilometres away due to the water supply disruption that has persisted for three weeks. Sahazudin Mohammad, 52, said he had to rely on R&R facilities to bathe despite living in his own home. “This water supply disruption has made daily life difficult for the residents, especially during Ramadan and the Aidilfitri celebrations.

items.title: World Bank Group's IFC, OCI TerraSus team up to advance Malaysia's semiconductor push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The World Bank Group, via its private-sector arm the International Finance Corporation (IFC), has partnered with OCI TerraSus Sdn Bhd (OCI TRS), a unit of South Korea's OCI Holdings Company Ltd, to advance Malaysia's participation in the global semiconductor supply chain and create jobs in the country. They said the partnership would also drive enhancements in IFC's environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards and management systems. OCI Holdings and OCI TerraSus chairman Lee Woo Hyun said the partnership is a key driver in strengthening management systems, ESG practices and long-term competitiveness as a global company.

items.title: Philippine peso hits record low, Indonesian rupiah steadies after support steps
items.summary: BENGALURU: The Philippine peso hit a record low on Monday and the Indonesian rupiah stopped short of touching a historic trough of 17,000 per dollar as the country's central bank introduced a new tool for banks to manage foreign-currency liquidity. The Philippine peso hit a low of 60.776 per dollar, while stocks in Manila tumbled 2.5 per cent, as the net oil importergrapples with an "imminent danger of a critically low energy supply." Bank Indonesia on Monday implemented new foreign exchange repo transactions using its FX-denominated securities, providing banks with an alternative for liquidity management, particularly foreign exchange liquidity.

items.title: Caretaker jailed over neglect linked to Baby Syifaa's death
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A caretaker was sentenced to one year's imprisonment by the Sessions Court here today for neglect that led to the death of 15-month-old Nour Rania Asyifaa Yuseri, known as Baby Syifaa, four years ago. The court also imposed a RM15,000 fine, with an additional four months' imprisonment in default. Baby Syifaa's parents, Anida Idrus, 44, and Yuseri Yusoff, 42, were present in court.

items.title: Hot weather boosts Harumanis quality, sweetness
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Prolonged hot weather in the northern peninsula has proven to be a 'bonus' for mango farmers, with this year's Harumanis harvest reported to be sweeter and of higher quality. Ahmad Ikhram, who also manages the Harumanis plantation, said this year's weather is ideal for producing premium fruit, with quality more consistent than in previous years. He said last year the plantation produced 17,000 Harumanis fruits, while this year the yield has increased to 30,000.

items.title: Taiping Lake Gardens water levels dip slightly, paddle boating continues
items.summary: TAIPING: Water levels at Taiping Lake Gardens remain largely stable, with only a slight decline observed. Taiping Municipal Council president Mohamed Akmal Dahalan said that recent rainfall had helped sustain the lake's level despite ongoing dry weather. Akmal said that recreational activities, including paddle boating, continued to operate as usual.

items.title: Farmers’ organisation board staff pleads not guilty to RM141,872 embezzlement in Johor Bahru
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 30 — A 36-year-old staff of the Farmers’ Organisation Board (LPP) claimed trial in the Sessions Court here today to 16 charges of fraudulently misappropriating property belonging to the Kota Tinggi Timur Farmers’ Organisation totalling RM141,872.67 over the past five years. Based on the charge sheets, the accused, who is also the Kota Tinggi Timur Area Farmers’ Organisation’s economic affairs assistant officer and manager, is charged with fraudulently embezzling proceeds from the sale of palm oil Fresh Fruit Bunches (FFB) totalling RM141,872.67 that belonged to the organisation. The accused was charged under Section 403 of the Penal Code for fraudulent misappropriation of property which carries a maximum prison sentence of five years, whipping and also a fine upon conviction.

items.title: Selangor exco calls for maximum jail and caning for fatal drink-driving cases
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 30 — A Selangor state executive councillor has called for tougher penalties, including maximum imprisonment and caning, for drink-driving offences following a fatal crash in Klang yesterday. State Human Resources and Poverty Eradication Committee chairman V. Papparaidu said existing measures appeared insufficient as a deterrent, as evidenced by the rising number of drink-driving cases. Yesterday, Klang Selatan district police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said a motorcyclist was killed after being hit by a car driven by an individual under the influence of alcohol and drugs on Jalan Raya Barat.

items.title: Drought leaves Kelantan padi farmers facing RM1mil in losses
items.summary: The affected farmers are from several villages, including Kampung Belukar, Kampung Salor and Kampung Beta Hilir. Farmers' representative Hafizul Daud from the Kemubu Agricultural Development Authority (Kada) Tanjung Puri said about 50 hectares of padi fields had been affected in what he described as the worst drought in six planting seasons. Hafizul added that farmers were also facing difficulties due to issues with water supply from the Kemubu irrigation system, forcing them to incur additional costs to draw water.

items.title: Unregulated houses of worship: 70pct of online comments AI-generated, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: About 70 per cent of online comments surrounding the recent unregulated houses of worship controversy were AI-generated, Deputy National Unity Minister Yuneswaran Ramaraj said, warning that the issue was being weaponised for political ends. Yuneswaran said the ministry had conducted research on the online discourse surrounding the temple issue and found the engagement patterns to be suspicious. But the engagement that morning was abnormally high," he said, adding that repetitive comments were another tell-tale sign of coordinated activity.

items.title: Ex-Army chief Hafizuddeain and wife to face joint trial over illegal proceeds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The 11-count case of receiving money from illegal activities involving former Army Chief (PTD) Tan Sri Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan and his wife, Salwani Anuar @ Kamaruddin will be tried together at the Sessions Court here. Earlier, Mahadi said Muhammad Hafizuddeain was previously charged at the Shah Alam Sessions Court and the case was transferred to the Sessions Court here, while Salwani was charged at the Kuala Terengganu Sessions Court and the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court. On January 22, Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan was charged at the Sessions Court here on four counts of receiving proceeds of illegal activities, namely money amounting to RM969,000, RM474,850, RM488,550 and RM190,000 which were deposited into three bank accounts belonging to him.

items.title: Foreign funds return to Bursa Malaysia, buck regional outflows
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Foreign investors turned net buyers last week, snapping a two-week streak of net selling with RM587.6 million in net inflows, according to MBSB Investment Bank Bhd. "The top three sectors that recorded net foreign inflows were plantation (RM385.9 million), financial services (RM295.6 million) and energy (RM139.1 million)," the bank said. Meanwhile, the sectors that saw the largest net foreign outflows were technology (RM123.1 million), consumer products and services (RM89.2 million) and construction (RM78.5 million).

items.title: Tony Fernandes: Demand defies fuel spikes, still better than Covid-19 grounding
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Low-cost carrier AirAsia is still seeing strong flight demand despite rising fuel prices that are affecting aviation companies globally. Capital A Bhd chief executive officer Tan Sri Tony Fernandes said the situation was "far worse" during Covid-19 as aircrafts were grounded. Fernandes said the strong demand enables the company to recover the extra costs as airline fares will have to go up.

items.title: Stable consumption, subsidies to support consumer sector outlook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The consumer products sector is expected to maintain earnings resilience and visibility, backed by stable domestic consumption and continued fiscal support. RHB Research said measures such as Sumbangan Asas Rahmah and targeted fuel subsidies should help sustain consumer spending amid rising global energy prices. RHB Research maintained its "Overweight" call on the sector, with Nestle (M) Bhd, Mr DIY Group (M) Bhd, Eco-Shop Marketing Bhd, Farm Fresh Bhd and Aeon Co (M) Bhd as its top picks.

items.title: Online Safety Act: A vital shield against digital harm
items.summary: LETTERS: Malaysia's Online Safety Act (ONSA), which came into force on Jan 1 this year, is not only timely but essential. Importantly, ONSA addresses a wide spectrum of harmful content (as per the First Schedule), including: * Child sexual abuse material, as provided under section 4 of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017; * Financial fraud-related content; obscene and indecent content that offends standards of decency; * Threatening, abusive, or insulting content causing harassment, fear, or distress; * Content that may incite violence or terrorism; * Content that encourages self-harm among children; * Content that promotes hostility, ill-will, or disrupts public tranquillity; and, * Content that promotes the use or sale of dangerous drugs. Among these categories, content that incites violence or supports terrorism remains particularly dangerous.

items.title: Eight charged over assault of three policemen at PPR
items.summary: Noor Nizar Shah Zainol Abidin, 37, Muhammad Syafiq Mohd Nordin, 28, Azwandy Azmi, 38, Muhamad Aiqal Zulkifrey, 28, Muhammad Firdaus Abdul Malik, 34, Muhammad Razin Abdul Muthalib, 34, Muhammad Syazwan Mohd Omar, 30, and Low Jia Hui, 26, claimed trial before magistrate Tunku Intan Nadiah Tunku Mohammed Nazarin. They are alleged to have caused injuries to Lance Corporal S. Uvaneand, 26, Constable Muhammad Daniel Danish Azlan, 23, and Lance Corporal Muhammad Amzaruddin Fakhri Khuiruddin, 30, while the trio were carrying out their duties in front of the PPR flats at Jalan Sungai at 9.50pm on March 25. Muhammad Firdaus, who has high blood pressure, and Muhammad Razin, whose wife is heavily pregnant, are both fathers.

items.title: Malaysians in Qatar urged to follow safety rules as schools reopen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Malaysian citizens in Qatar are advised to always comply with the safety instructions and guidelines issued by Qatari authorities following the reopening of schools in the country. “With the reopening of schools, all Malaysians in Qatar are advised to constantly comply with the instructions and safety guidelines set by the Qatari authorities,” according to a statement uploaded on the Embassy’s Facebook page on Sunday. Qatar’s Ministry of Education and Higher Education announced last week the gradual resumption of in-person classes across educational institutions in the country starting March 24, with full in-person attendance for all students in schools and kindergartens resuming on March 29, Qatar News Agency (QNA) reported.

items.title: Online learning won't affect students' progress, says Wong Kah Woh
items.summary: TAIPING: The Education Ministry has assured that students' learning will not be disrupted if schools need to switch to online lessons due to the current hot weather. Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said that while no cases of heat stroke among students had been reported so far, schools and authorities were prepared to implement Home-Based Teaching and Learning (PdPR) if necessary. Even last year, when a storm struck Selangor, several schools conducted PdPR for over a month without issue.

items.title: Racism cases in Malaysia hit record high in 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Racism-related incidents in Malaysia hit a record high of 107 cases in 2025, marking the highest level in more than a decade, according to a new report. "In 2025, we recorded 107 cases, the highest number in the eleven years since the start of this initiative," the report said. The Malaysia Racism Report 2025 by Pusat Komas found that cases were largely driven by developments in public discourse and policy (45 cases), followed by Parliament (43 cases) and social settings (19 cases).

items.title: Penang courts tourists from China, India, Asean to offset Middle East and European shortfall
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 30 — Penang is looking to attract more tourists from China, India and neighbouring countries in South-east Asia to make up for the shortfall in visitors from the Middle East and European countries. Penang Tourism and Creative Economy Committee chairman Wong Hon Wai said currently, two airlines are still grounded in Doha and Dubai due to the conflict in Middle East so it will affect tourist arrivals from the Middle East as well as Europeans. He said there are increasing direct flights from China such as the inaugural flight from Qingdao Airline from northern China.

items.title: Govt exploring National Harmony Commission, law to tackle racial tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is in the early stages of establishing a National Harmony Commission and drafting a Harmony Act, Deputy National Unity Minister Yuneswaran Ramaraj said. "We are in the midst of discussions to have a National Harmony Commission. Yuneswaran said legislation in the form of a Harmony Act was also being considered.

items.title: Two houses, surau destroyed in Keningau fire
items.summary: KENINGAU: Two houses and a surau were destroyed in a fire at Kampung Pagar-Pagar Sondoton here late last night. Acting Keningau Fire and Rescue Station officer Evan Bobby said the team received an emergency call at 12.53am, before a 10-member crew was dispatched to the scene. "Upon arrival at 1am, the fire had engulfed two houses and a surau completely," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Malaysians in Qatar advised to follow safety guidelines amid school reopening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian citizens in Qatar are advised to adhere strictly to safety instructions and guidelines issued by Qatari authorities following the reopening of schools in the country. "With the reopening of schools, all Malaysians in Qatar are advised to consistently follow the instructions and safety guidelines set by Qatari authorities," it said in a statement posted on its Facebook page yesterday. Qatar's Ministry of Education and Higher Education announced last week the gradual resumption of in-person classes across the country's educational institutions from March 24, with full attendance for all students in schools and kindergartens resuming on March 29, according to Qatar News Agency (QNA).

items.title: Malaysia poised to attract tourists amid Middle East crisis
items.summary: State Tourism, Industry, Investment and Corridor Development Committee chairman Loh Sze Yee said while no country was immune to the spillover effects of the crisis, Malaysia was in a relatively favourable position due to its stability and ample energy supply. Loh said that several countries were grappling with rising fuel prices and supply shortages, which have disrupted travel and increased operational costs. "Malaysia is fortunate.

items.title: [UPDATED] Blogger Papagomo fined RM4,000 for seditious remarks against King
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Blogger Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, also known as Papagomo, was fined RM4,000 for making seditious statements that incited hatred against His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia. Sessions Court Judge Norma Ismail handed down the sentence after the accused failed to raise reasonable doubt against the prosecution's case today. Lawyer Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali, representing Wan Muhammad Azri, requested a lenient sentence, citing that his client had five children to support, including an autistic child.

items.title: #NSTviral: Motorist rear-ended after stopping for police escort sparks debate online [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A viral video of a car being rear-ended after stopping at a traffic light for a police motorcycle escort has sparked debate among netizens over liability in such situations. The short clip, widely circulated on social media, shows a vehicle coming to a stop at a junction, believed to be in compliance with instructions from a police outrider managing traffic. In the footage, the police outrider only comes into view after the collision, dismounting his motorcycle before proceeding to direct traffic at the junction.

items.title: Racist remarks in Parliament 'deeply saddening', says Yuneswaran
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Deputy National Unity Minister Yuneswaran Ramaraj has expressed concern over racist remarks made by members of parliament, after a report identified Parliament as the largest source of recorded incidents last year. Speaking at the launch of Pusat Komas' Malaysia Racism Report 2025 at the Sultan Abdul Samad Building today, he said he was "very sad" to see such remarks coming from fellow parliamentarians. Yuneswaran said the matter would be raised with Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul.

items.title: Police search for mother of newborn abandoned in Ampang rubbish bin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are still searching for the mother of a newborn baby girl found abandoned in a rubbish bin outside a house in Taman Putra, Ampang, last Wednesday. Ampang Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Khairul Anuar Khalid, said the woman, believed to be the infant's mother, had yet to be located. "The suspect, who is the baby's mother, is still being traced.

items.title: Trump: Iran sending US '20 big boats of oil'
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Sunday that Iran is sending the United States (US) 20 oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday morning, Anadolu Ajansi reported. "And now, today, they gave us, as a tribute -- I don't know, I can't define it exactly, but they gave us, I think out of a sign of respect, 20 boats of oil -- big, big boats of oil -- going through the Hormuz Strait, and that's taking place starting tomorrow morning," he told reporters aboard Air Force One en route to Washington, DC. Trump also said the US is negotiating with Iran both "directly and indirectly."

items.title: Federal Court decision could determine future of Shah Alam Community Forest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The fate of the Shah Alam Community Forest (SACF) is set to be decided by the Federal Court, which will rule on a legal challenge over its status as part of a forest reserve. According to The Star , the decision could directly affect a contentious road project cutting through the SACF, which has faced strong opposition from residents and environmental groups. Selangor public health and environment committee chairman Jamaliah Jamaluddin said the state government had yet to take an official position, with the matter expected to be discussed at the next state executive council (MMKN) meeting.

items.title: King briefed on Online Safety Act 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, was briefed today on the Online Safety Act 2025 at Istana Bukit Tunku. According to a post on His Majesty's official Facebook account, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil presented details of the Act. Fahmi said the Act also seeks to address the issue of fake accounts targeting the Malay Rulers and the Royal Family.

items.title: G7 ministers set to tackle financial fallout of Mideast war
items.summary: PARIS, France: Ministers from the G7 will hold talks on Monday to unpack the economic consequences of the war in the Middle East, the French government said, as oil and gas prices continue to soar. The squeeze on supply has pushed oil and natural gas prices higher, with drastic knock-on effects for supply chains in countless industries. French Finance Minister Roland Lescure said the G7 meeting, to be held via videoconference, would include energy and finance ministers as well as central bank chiefs and the heads of other international agencies.

items.title: #NSTviral: Motorcyclist's bid to outrun traffic police ends in swift arrest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A video showing a young motorcyclist's failed attempt to evade traffic police has gone viral, drawing both criticism and amusement from netizens. The 60-second clip captures the moment a rider in a green T-shirt is flagged down by a traffic officer at a busy junction. Although he appears to comply, the rider suddenly revs his engine and speeds off as the officer turns to manage traffic.

items.title: Australia to halve tax on fuel, underwrite spot cargoes
items.summary: PERTH/SYDNEY: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said on Monday the government would halve the excise on fuel and diesel and remove the heavy road user charge for three months to help households cope with a surge in costs driven by the Iran war. Halving the tax would reduce the cost of fuel by 26.3 Australian cents per litre, Albanese said. The total cost to the government would be about A$2.55 billion (US$1.75 billion), Treasurer Jim Chalmers said at a joint press conference with the prime minister and Energy Minister Chris Bowen.

items.title: King receives update on Online Safety Act to shield children from social media risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia, was today presented with the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA 2025) during a royal audience at Istana Bukit Tunku, according to a post on the King’s official Facebook page. Among its key measures, the act introduces clear responsibilities for online platforms and implements an age verification mechanism to prevent children under 16 from opening social media accounts, safeguarding their safety online. Fahmi also highlighted that ONSA 2025 seeks to address fake accounts targeting the Malay Rulers and royal family members.

items.title: Driver in his 20s remanded four days after fatal Klang crash hurls motorcyclist onto car roof
items.summary: KLANG, March 30 — A man has been remanded for four days from today for allegedly driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs and causing the death of a motorcyclist on Jalan Raya Barat here yesterday. Yesterday, Klang Selatan Police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said the incident, which occurred at about 11.40am, caused the victim, a man in his 30s, to be flung onto the roof of another car. He said initial investigations found that a Honda City driven by a man heading towards Klang town was being dangerously driven at high speed.

items.title: King meets PM Anwar to discuss federal administration, current issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia, today received Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim in a formal audience at Istana Bukit Tunku. According to a post on the King’s official Facebook page, the meeting included updates on the federal government’s administration and discussions on current issues. The audience was held at the palace earlier today (March 30).

items.title: Massive 5.5m python caught feasting on calf in Baling oil palm plantation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — A 5.5-metre python was spotted swallowing a calf at a palm oil plantation near Paya Keladi in Baling, Kedah, yesterday. According to the owner, Wan Hasbullah Wan Ismail, 57, the calf went missing in the afternoon while grazing with its mother in the area. Baling Civil Defence officer Leftenan (PA) Mohd Faizol Abd Aziz said the team received the call at 8.20pm.

items.title: Caught on camera: Suspected parcel thief steals from Telok Panglima Garang home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — A suspected parcel thief was caught on camera in Telok Panglima Garang after a video of the incident went viral on social media. The footage, shared on X by user @update1111, was reported by the New Straits Times and included a warning to residents: “Be careful, there is already a thief in the Telok Panglima Garang/Jalan Kebun area.” According to the clip, the incident took place on March 29 at 7.56am. CCTV footage showed a man and a woman arriving on a motorcycle and stopping in front of a house with its main gate open.

items.title: Civil Defence captures six‑foot crocodile lurking in Sarawak housing drain after residents raise alarm
items.summary: SARIKEI, March 30 — A crocodile measuring about six feet long (1.83 metres) was captured by Civil Defence Force (APM) personnel at a housing area here on Saturday. APM Sarikei officer Shahrol Azizi Zaidi said the reptile was caught at about 6pm in a drain near a home in Taman Susur Jambu. “The sighting of the crocodile triggered safety concerns, prompting the residents to contact APM to assist in capturing the reptile,” he said.

items.title: Malaysian drivers in Thailand beware: Steep fines and jail await those who flout traffic laws from April
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Malaysian motorists who violate traffic laws in Thailand will face heavier penalties from April, including fines of up to RM2,385.41 (20,000 baht) and a maximum prison term of one year. Offences covered include speeding, running red lights, failing to stop at pedestrian crossings, using mobile phones without hands-free devices, driving against traffic, and not wearing helmets for riders and passengers. Other violations include failing to wear seatbelts, driving without a licence, driving under the influence of alcohol, and reckless driving.

items.title: Perak warns against open burning as hot, dry conditions push fire cases to worrying levels
items.summary: IPOH, March 30 — A total of 700 open burning cases involving vegetation were recorded in Perak from January to March 2026, making it the highest category each month. State Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Sandrea Ng Shy Ching said the current hot and dry spell has further increased the risk of open burning incidents in the state. “In addition to bush and grass fires, the state Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) reported 157 cases of garbage fires during the same period.” “Meanwhile, there were 53 bush fire cases, while plantation or farm fires accounted for 47 cases,” she said at a press conference after the closing ceremony of a public awareness programme in conjunction with the Perak JBPM Madani Rakyat programme here yesterday.

items.title: Follow the law, not pressure, says Sarawak minister on Bumiputera status
items.summary: KUCHING, March 30 — There is no need to unnecessarily prolong the debate on Bumiputera status, as the matter cannot be determined through public pressure or casual suggestions, said Minister of Tourism, Creative Industry and Performing Arts Datuk Seri Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah. Abdul Karim, who is also Minister of Youth, Sports and Entrepreneur Development, said any move to alter the definition of Bumiputera or native status in Sarawak must follow proper legal and constitutional process, noting that clear provisions are already in place. “Where one spouse is a native of Sabah or Sarawak, their child is eligible to acquire native status and related rights, including land ownership.

items.title: Discovery of baby’s skeleton in Alor Gajah: Remand of five extended for three more days
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The remand of five individuals, including a family of four, in connection with the discovery of a baby’s skeleton buried in front of a house in Kelemak in Alor Gajah, Melaka, has been extended by three days. Melaka police chief, Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar, said the court granted the remand extension for all suspects, aged between 16 and 40, until Wednesday, according to Kosmo! The four family members include a 19-year-old girl believed to be the mother of the baby, her mother, sister, brother, and the girl’s boyfriend.

items.title: Chow Kon Yeow says Penang’s Unity Government proves stronger together as Pakatan and BN leaders unite
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 30 — Close cooperation between Pakatan Harapan (PH) and component parties in the Unity Government form the basis of the coalition’s continued stability and strength. Penang PH leadership council chairman Chow Kon Yeow said the approach is in line with the principle of “stronger together”, which is able to strengthen the position of all parties involved ahead of the general election and state election. Chow pointed out that cooperation under the Unity Government has proven to be an effective formula in facing elections in Penang.

items.title: Running red lights, using phones while driving remain top traffic offences in Selangor, JPJ finds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Running red lights and using mobile phones while driving were among the most common traffic offences recorded during the 2026 Hari Raya Aidilfitri Special Operation by the Selangor Road Transport Department (JPJ). The operation, conducted from March 13 to March 29, monitored roads intensively across the state and detected a variety of traffic violations, according to a report in Sinar Harian today. Selangor JPJ director, Azrin Borhan, said these two offences were the most frequently committed by road users during the operation.

items.title: Minister says Haj preparations on track as team inspects facilities in Mecca and Medina
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Preparations for this year’s Haj season are progressing smoothly and according to plan, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Datuk Dr Zulkifli Hasan said. He said the visit was also used to inspect facilities and accommodation for prospective pilgrims in Mecca, including those for Tabung Haji personnel. “Alhamdulillah, everything is progressing smoothly as planned,” he said.

items.title: Rain showers cool parts of Kedah, but hot spell continues as MetMalaysia keeps watch on rising heat
items.summary: Based on the department’s updated data, the entire Perlis and nine districts in Kedah, however, recorded Level 1 hot weather or alert status. The districts involved in Kedah are Sik, Padang Terap, Pendang, Pokok Sena, Kubang Pasu, Pulau Langkawi, Kota Setar, Kuala Muda and Baling. Yesterday, several areas in Kedah began to receive rain, including the Kota Setar, Kubang Pasu and Kuala Muda districts, bringing relief to local residents.

items.title: Strong stench leads to discovery of man’s body in Ipoh home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The body of a 52-year-old man was found in a house in Taman Kledang Jaya in Ipoh yesterday after neighbours alerted police to a foul odour coming from the property. Ipoh District Police Chief, ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah, said the man, a local resident, was discovered at around 11.26am following the tip-off, according to a report published by Astro Awani. “The deceased had a history of chronic illnesses, including diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and had previously suffered a mild stroke.

items.title: High-profile court cases on sexual offences and police custody death heat up KL, Putrajaya this week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The spotlight this week falls on three high-profile legal proceedings in Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya, including cases involving influencers and artists facing sexual offences, as well as the 2019 death of a grave digger in police custody. In the appeal, the government and PDRM raised two legal questions for the court’s consideration. Previously, the Court of Appeal upheld the High Court in George Town’s May 2024 decision, which found both plaintiffs accountable for the death.

items.title: KL’s secret LRT tunnel doubles as emergency evacuation route for passengers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Hidden beneath the Jalan Ampang Islamic Cemetery and often linked to local folklore, the tunnel connecting Kampung Baru and KLCC LRT stations plays a critical role as the primary evacuation route during emergencies or underground accidents. Five of its 37 stations — KLCC, Kampung Baru, Dang Wangi, Masjid Jamek, and Pasar Seni — are underground. Syahrul Anuar Abd Wahab, Section Chief of the Rapid Rail Facilities Department, said one tunnel at Kewalram links Kampung Baru and KLCC, while the other is located at Jalan Munshi Abdullah.

items.title: Chief Justice leads glittering roll of 1,026 Sabah award recipients as state celebrates Governor’s birthday
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 30 — Chief Justice Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh heads the list of 1,026 recipients of Sabah state honours, awards and medals in conjunction with the official birthday celebration of Yang Dipertua Negeri of Sabah Tun Musa Aman today. Wan Ahmad Farid is among the three recipients of the Seri Panglima Darjah Kinabalu (SPDK), Sabah’s highest honour, which carries the title “Datuk Seri Panglima”. Other recipients are Royal Malaysia Police Secretary DCP Abdul Rahman Kassim, Private Secretary to Sabah Governor Izharudin Jalaludin, Sabah Islamic Affairs Department (JHEAINS) Director Awang Juri Awang Tahir and Sabah Electricity Sdn Bhd Chairperson Saadiah Aziz.

items.title: Police: Egyptian tenant hauled in after threatening to torch Bukit Jalil flat and kill elderly landlady
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Police have arrested an Egyptian man for allegedly threatening to kill an elderly woman and burn down her house at the Kampung Muhibbah People’s Housing Project (PPR), Bukit Jalil, on Wednesday. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the suspect, in his 20s, who was renting a room at the victim’s house, was detained at the premises early Thursday after a report was lodged by the woman, in her mid-70s. In a statement today, he said preliminary investigations indicate the suspect acted following a dispute over monthly rental payments.

items.title: Police probe deadly Klang smash after Honda City driver, allegedly intoxicated, collides head-on with motorcyclist
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 30 — A motorcyclist was killed after being hit by a driver believed to be under the influence of alcohol and drugs in an accident along Jalan Raya Barat in Klang near here yesterday. South Klang police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said the incident, which occurred at about 11.40am, caused the male victim in his 30s to be thrown onto another car. He said initial investigations found that a Honda City driven by a local man in his 20s heading towards Klang town was being dangerously driven at high speed.

items.title: AI travel advice backfires: Israelis detained at KLIA during transit, envoy warns against Malaysia trips
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — At least eight Israeli nationals have been detained at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) in recent months, including some holding dual citizenship, according to a report by Ynetnews. The group comprised travellers either seeking entry into Malaysia or transiting through the country en route to other destinations in Asia. Malaysia does not have diplomatic relations with Israel.
